raw:
```json
{
  "score": 3,
  "anchor": 3,
  "reasons": [
    "OpenAPI is valid (swagger-cli confirms) and complete with all 13 endpoints documented across books, members, loans, reservations, and fines resources.",
    "URL design follows REST conventions consistently — nouns, plural, hierarchical (e.g. /api/loans/{id}/return, /api/members/{id}/reservations) — with no verb-based paths.",
    "All request bodies are validated with Zod in both representative handlers (loan-handler.ts:9, reservation-handler.ts:9); invalid input returns 400 with field-level details.",
    "Status codes in the spec match the implementation: 201 for creation, 200 for success, 404 for not found, 402/403/409 for domain-specific errors (probe confirms these).",
    "Error shape is consistent: {\"error\": \"<message>\"} as the base form, documented in the OpenAPI Error schema, with a details array appended for validation failures.",
    "No centralized error-handling middleware exists; error-to-HTTP mapping is duplicated via instanceof chains in each handler (loan-handler.ts:21-35, reservation-handler.ts:21-31) rather than being read in one place.",
    "Error responses lack a stable machine-readable code field (e.g. \"LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED\") for programmatic handling.",
    "OpenAPI does not include examples for any negative acceptance criteria responses (402, 403, 409) — the operations describe status codes but no example payloads."
  ]
}
```

reasons: OpenAPI is valid (swagger-cli confirms) and complete with all 13 endpoints documented across books, members, loans, reservations, and fines resources.
URL design follows REST conventions consistently — nouns, plural, hierarchical (e.g. /api/loans/{id}/return, /api/members/{id}/reservations) — with no verb-based paths.
All request bodies are validated with Zod in both representative handlers (loan-handler.ts:9, reservation-handler.ts:9); invalid input returns 400 with field-level details.
Status codes in the spec match the implementation: 201 for creation, 200 for success, 404 for not found, 402/403/409 for domain-specific errors (probe confirms these).
Error shape is consistent: {"error": "<message>"} as the base form, documented in the OpenAPI Error schema, with a details array appended for validation failures.

No centralized error-handling middleware exists; error-to-HTTP mapping is duplicated via instanceof chains in each handler (loan-handler.ts:21-35, reservation-handler.ts:21-31) rather than being read in one place.
Error responses lack a stable machine-readable code field (e.g. "LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED") for programmatic handling.
OpenAPI does not include examples for any negative acceptance criteria responses (402, 403, 409) — the operations describe status codes but no example payloads.
